chunkText: Purpose: Support consistent review of infliximab therapeutic drug monitoring cases across the hospital network.
Escalation criteria: Escalate when subtherapeutic trough level is combined with suspected loss of response, positive anti-drug antibodies, high inflammatory markers, or missing critical context.
Required data: Include last dose date, trough timing, measured level, anti-drug antibodies, CRP, albumin, clinical activity score, and treatment history.
AI use guardrail: AI may summarize, retrieve protocol sections, identify missing data, and draft documentation. Final clinical decision requires expert validation.
Purpose: Standardize review of adalimumab monitoring across satellite hospitals.
Escalation criteria: Escalate when trough level is below target, anti-drug antibodies are present, adherence uncertainty exists, or clinical activity is worsening.
Required data: Include dosing interval, last administration, level timing, antibodies, CRP, clinical score, and adherence notes.
AI use guardrail: AI output is a draft and must not be treated as a prescription or diagnosis.
Purpose: Provide a consistent workflow for ustekinumab PK/PD review.
Escalation criteria: Escalate if drug level is low with persistent activity, complex comedication, or unresolved interpretation.
Required data: Include weight, last dose date, measured level, CRP, albumin, disease activity and previous biologic exposure.
AI use guardrail: AI may compare cases and protocols but does not autonomously recommend therapy.
Purpose: Help hospitals identify high-risk vancomycin monitoring cases requiring expert review.
Escalation criteria: Escalate when levels are outside target, kidney function worsens, timing of sampling is uncertain, or patient is critically ill.
Required data: Include dose schedule, administration times, sample time, drug level, creatinine trend, weight, renal replacement therapy status, and infection context.
AI use guardrail: AI may prepare a case packet and retrieve protocol excerpts. Dosing decisions remain under clinician responsibility.
Purpose: Coordinate interpretation of tacrolimus levels in patients with variability or toxicity risk.
Escalation criteria: Escalate if trough levels are repeatedly outside range, interacting medicines are present, or renal toxicity risk increases.
Required data: Include trough timing, dose, interacting drugs, creatinine, transplantation or immunosuppression context, and toxicity symptoms.
AI use guardrail: AI may extract comedications and summarize history but cannot approve final dose changes.
Purpose: Support antifungal monitoring, especially in complex oncology or immunosuppressed patients.
Escalation criteria: Escalate when levels are below target, above safety range, liver enzymes rise, or relevant CYP interactions are identified.
Required data: Include dose, timing, level, ALT, AST, bilirubin, comedications, infection indication and absorption concerns.
AI use guardrail: AI may detect missing information and draft review notes. Expert review is required for clinical action.
Indications and usage: Synthetic demo section for Infliximab.
Monitoring considerations: Synthetic demo section to be replaced with official label and local protocol content.
Indications and usage: Synthetic demo section for Adalimumab.
